韓國股市持續上升，原因是外資開始回流，同時總統李明博也對市場表示了更強有力的支持。這兩個因素必須持續存在，才能讓周四的股市反彈具有持續性。在周三韓國股市創歷史新低時，海外投資者反而大量進入市場。
他們在市場劇烈下跌的背景下以約9.57億美元購入了這些股份，當時基準指數Kospi下跌了12%，這是該指數自1980年創立以來最大幅度的跌幅。海外投資者以前所未有的速度在Kosdaq市場購買小型公司的股票。週三的市場崩盤可能已經重新調整了股價，為海外投資者提供了尋找買入機會的機會。
周四開市時，Kospi 指數累計上漲 12%，這顯示國內投資者可能也加入了這波上漲的行列。許多韓國零售投資者將李視為一種民間英雄。總統不斷鼓勵公眾改變投資策略，轉向金融資產；最近他甚至將自己的公寓出租，以便用所得資金購買股票。
若外資持續流入，並配合穩定及其他措施，紀錄上最長的兩日賣壓——韓國股市Kospi在周二和周三下跌超過18%——或許不會被視為結構性退出，而是被視為政策支持的短期下跌，值得買入。
如果出售這些履歷，那將會挑戰最近的觀點，也就是所謂的“韓國折扣”現象——即韓國指數（Kospi）的估值遠低於其大多數同行——已經接近終結了。

💥 尽管今日的30年期國債發行較為成功，但長期日本國債（JGB）的收益率上壓持續。那些期待伊朗衝突能迅速解決的債券投資者，如果能源價格長期居高不下，將會遭受損失，因為這會對長期JGB造成負面影響。從估值角度來看，收益率仍有上升的空間。目前十年期JGB的收益率約比1月創下的周期高點低20個基點。
同時，30年期利率仍維持在約46基點以下。僅幾週前，日本利率因預期通膨將由緩和的央行緊縮循環來緩解而快速上揚。一個證明比樂觀預期更持久的能源衝擊將進一步強化這種利率動態，使曲線更加陡峭。

💥 亞太央行認為伊朗衝突的持續時間是關鍵變數，人們如此說道
投資者似乎將美國和以色列對伊朗的戰爭視為一個短期風險事件，類似於特朗普總統任內曾經出現的那些風險事件。這種輕率的態度意味着，如果石油價格持續高企或隨著衝突的持續而進一步上漲，全球股市——尤其是美國股市——將有遭受大幅下跌的風險。
化石燃料的風險與收益比例嚴重失衡，價格呈上升趨勢——儘管美國承諾恢復霍爾木兹水道的通航，原油價格仍在周四大幅上漲。目前尚無明確的時間表來結束這場衝突，而且即便爭議得到解決，伊朗的局勢可能依然會持續混亂，這將導致該國石油供應及通過這條關鍵水道的交通狀況持續處於不確定狀態。
每一天的停產狀態都會增加油井被關閉的風險，未來重新啟動將需要時間。與2022年有明顯的相似之處，目前原油的走勢正是那條路線，不過當時俄羅斯在國際油市中佔有較大的比重，與現在伊朗的比重相比，這導致當前原油的收益進步較為緩慢，漲幅僅為25%。布倫特原油價格從12月起上漲了25%。
2021年31日，即烏克蘭戰爭於2月23日爆發前夕，該指數在當年已上漲19%；而在過去週末的衝擊之前，其漲幅更是達到了19%。雖然2026年的增長速度落後於2022年，但兩者之間的差距仍相當明顯。截至目前，布倫特原油的價格已上漲約40%，這與2022年的持續上漲幅度相當。投資者應當警惕烏克蘭戰爭所帶來的影響，以及美國2003年入侵伊拉克的歷史經驗。
衝突的持續時間可能遠超預期；而當我們在2026年進入這一年時，油價將會處在企業原本預計的高水平之上。人們之所以會在股價下跌時蜂擁購買，是基於過去股價突然下跌後買進者所獲得的收益經驗。然而，油價和中東地區的長期發展趨勢，加上近期的歷史經驗，讓人們需要保持一定的謹慎。

澳洲的債券交易員在中央銀行本月的政策審議之前，與從事現貨利率及未來利率交易的同行們的立場似乎大相徑庭。
三年期債券的殖利率自10月以來持續上升，本周升勢更加明顯。總裁米契爾·布洛克發表特別強硬的言論，警告稱3月17日的會議將會有實質性決定，這打破了澳洲聯邦銀行僅在每季度才進行調整的觀念。儘管她發出了這樣的警告，現金利率期貨仍預計本週只會出現小幅升息。
雖然澳洲央行（RBA）有可能維持利率不變，但我認為實際發生利率變動的機會遠高於期貨市場所預測的24%。債券曲線中3年期與10年期部分的利差已從一年前的超過100基點大幅縮小至現在的43基點，這與全球債券市場的發展趨勢相符。債券曲線的進一步扁平化可能要等到澳洲央行本月的決定後才能實現。
若油價持續上漲，儘管市場情緒有所改善，這將加壓於澳洲聯邦銀行（RBA）實施連續利率提升。
💥 開戰地區的布倫特原油期貨價格已接近每桶 85 美元，並在週二的盤中達到歷史高點。由於中東地區的衝突沒有緩和的跡象，油價持續上升。在過去一個小時內，以色列對伊朗發起了新的攻擊，而伊朗此前也對美國和以色列的目標發射了導彈和無人機。這種不穩定狀況正在全球範圍內擴散，中國政府已要求該國最大的煉油企業暫停柴油和汽油的出口。
風險情緒進一步趨緩，這與慣常模式一致——Stoxx 50 期貨跌至新低點，油價創新高。美國股價期貨也繼續下跌，亞洲市場在後期階段逐步收窄收益，尤其是恒生指數，目前已經持平。
歐洲股市除了受到經濟因素影響外，還面臨歐洲天然氣期貨開盤於高位的額外風險。交易員們對本周價格上漲的反應是持樂觀態度，並進行了看漲的政策押注，這些押注進一步削弱了該地區股市的吸引力。美元依舊是投資者的避風港，其漲勢在最新市場動態中得以持續。
黃金丟失了當日的漲幅，銀和銅價則跌入負值區間，貴金屬整體表現不佳，未能提升其作為避險資產的聲譽。美國10年期國債價格下跌，使得其收益率上升了2個基點，達到4.12%。以下是您今天早晨需要了解的五個重要消息：歐洲情況。
杜拜股市下跌 4.1%，為自 2020 年 3 月以來最大兩日跌勢
歐洲的債券今日仍面臨下跌風險，儘管過夜市場情緒有所改善。由於除了市場氛圍外，沒有其他明顯因素促使交易者購買債券，因此前端證券的收益率很可能會再次上升。
布倫特原油價格再次上漲，且漲幅遠超近期水平。如果衝突持續下去，中央銀行此前根據較低的石油和天然氣價格所做出的通膨預測，很快就會變得不再準確。
雖然英國的過夜索償合約已經不再預期本月會降息，但這些合約仍考慮到英國央行在今年餘下時間可能會實施約35基點的貨幣寬鬆政策。即便巴以衝突再持續幾周，能源供應的擾亂也可能持續足夠長的時間，從而使通貨膨脹在全年保持高位。
英國銀行（BOE）認為目前的經濟環境並不適合降息，因此對貨幣政策放鬆的樂觀態度似乎有些不切實際。相比之下，歐洲中央銀行的利率設定更加符合現實情況。雖然市場目前尚未認為今年會有升息的機會，但也不應該預期會有降息。鑑於歐元區的通貨膨脹已經得到有效控制，這種預期是合理的。然而，我們仍處在債券市場調整的初期階段。
在日間之外，也存在一種統一的平坦化趨勢，我在此已進行過探討。

油價下挫的趨勢正在快速消退，歐洲自然氣價開盤上漲約 9%。兩者都在推動歐洲區債券殖利率上漲，而投資者則持續加碼對今年歐洲央行（ECB）強化政策的押注。不過，他們仍應謹慎看待後者過度加碼的風險。
我們已經看到歐洲央行（ECB）對未來利率走勢的預測發生快速變化，市場從年初認為年底有約 40% 的降息可能性，轉而認為升息的可能性高達 34%。這是因為交易員們對能源成本上升可能帶來的通膨影響感到擔憂。自然而然地，這一情況會讓人聯想到 2022 年俄烏戰爭期間的經歷——當時歐洲央行為應對能源成本激增，實施了 450 基點（bps）的緊縮政策。
然而，當時歐洲央行（ECB）的存款利率設定在-0.5%，這表明貨幣政策已經相當寬鬆，無法有效應對如此巨大的價格衝擊，因此需要進行一定的調整以追上通貨膨脹的發展。四年後，存款利率上升至2%。在此之前，這一利率在某些方面被認為過於嚴格，歐洲央行存在未能實現其通貨膨脹目標的風險。
即使本周五年的通膨掉期利率有所回升，市場對歐洲央行（ECB）的通脹管理目標已經持接近的態度，並未明顯超出其目標範圍。此外，歐洲央行理事會在考慮是否要進一步加強對經濟增長衝擊的應對措施時也持謹慎態度；而歐洲央行主席Villeroy最近的言論也表明他們並不急於採取行動。
因此，雖然市場預期歐洲央行（ECB）今年不會再進行降息是合理的，但對於明確升息的押注可能會對目前的資訊發展產生過度反應。

法國債券若伊朗衝突持續，將特別易受賣壓影響。近期法國利差隨即收窄，主要因缺乏負面新聞驅動，而非基本面出現明顯改善。低波動性與歐洲央行表示在可預見的未來將維持 2% 的利率水平，這促使投資人積極買入法國債券，利差進一步收窄至 2024 年選舉前的水平。
這種背景如今已處於風險之中。伊朗的衝突使得歐洲央行（ECB）可能採取更加激進的貨幣政策。同時，更高的能源成本將對經濟增長造成壓力，進一步惡化法國的財政狀況。這種貿易失衡與通貨緊縮並存的局面對高度負債的國家來說並不有利。當市場開始關注財政問題時，往往會首先對那些有過財政失誤紀錄的國家造成影響。
法國在財政紀律方面的表現，包括在俄烏能源衝突期間對家庭提供大量直接支持，進一步強化了其脆弱性。若能源價格持續高漲，政治壓力重複類似措施的可能性將增加，這將進一步加壓法國的財政狀況。
進入這場危機時，市場預測2026年的財政赤字將佔GDP的約5.3%，遠超歐洲委員會設定的3%的目標。貨幣市場的借貸活動需要穩定的風險情緒和低波動性作爲基礎。雖然目前市場波動仍相對較小，但法國債券收益率的風險卻明顯上升。

日本最大的勞工團體「Rengo」正要求工資成長率達到近 6%，這遠高於與日本央行（BOJ）穩定物價目標相符合的水平。而代表較小企業的勞工團體則提出了更高的要求。日本央行在抑制通膨方面已明顯落後於其他國家，其現行的基準利率僅為 0.75%，與中性利率的預測值相差約 75 基點。
但鑑於政府的額外支出、可能會導致的今年薪資上漲，以及原油價格持續上漲的預期，央行將不得不將利率大幅提高，遠超中性水平。而日本央行（BOJ）如果拖延行動，對日圓債券的影響將更加負面。政策制定者將在本月底會面，但據我們的同事報告，近期幾乎不可能立即採取任何行動。

歐洲股市開盤下跌，Stoxx 600 指數下跌 0.4%。正如 Conor 今日所提到的，該地區股市的走勢在很大程度上取決於能源產業的發展。在伊朗對霍爾木茲海峽的評論引發市場關注後，交易員們迅速放棄了對原油價格的做空策略，這表明目前原油價格更有可能上漲而非下跌。在這種情況下，歐洲股市的任何反彈都難以持續。
在利率領域，能源價格的上漲影響仍在持續，歐洲各期限國債的收益率普遍上升。十年期英國國債的收益率開盤時已比上周四月高出約六個基點，因為交易員們繼續將英國央行降息的時間點推遲到更遠的未來。下一次降息目前尚未完全被市場預期到，時間點已從上週的四月變為十一月。
對英國央行（BOE）的購買價格影響極為顯著，這與該委員會內部的分歧有關——在本次事件發生前，就已有多名成員對鬆動政策持保留態度。對於這些成員，以及那些可能支持降息的人來說，即便能源價格在短期內有所下降，但本周價格的大幅上漲仍表明英國經濟脫離通貨膨脹的軌道仍不確定，且容易受到各種因素的干擾。

市場基準的聯邦儲備系統利率預估在年底將大幅上揚，目前已達到九個月來的高點。美國債券收益率曾大幅下跌，但這種趨勢已經逆轉。對美國來說，這場危機在經濟條件並不特別緊縮的情況下就已經化解了。對亞洲及其他發展中國家來說，任何美元走強的跡象都可能成為財政困難的挑戰。

我對今天這項行動的影響仍持懷疑態度，認為它將進一步擴大，考慮到日本整體的宏觀經濟形勢。雖然目前的原油價格已有所收縮，但其上漲趨勢仍可能進一步壓制選後的樂觀情緒，因交易員擔心政府可能採取更強硬的財政政策。此外，這也增加了日本央行（BOJ）難以在近期緊縮政策的風險，因潛在的經濟成長衝擊可能影響其政策調整。
總裁上田今天已經警告指出，如果經濟發展與中央銀行的預測出現偏差，銀行的立場將會改變。雖然美國股權期貨價格有所上升，但今日的漲勢顯得非常不穩定。一旦這種上升勢頭受到打擊，美元/日元的匯率走勢很可能会重新回到本周的交易模式中，美元將再次成為投資者尋求的避風港。
USD/JPY is near session lows as a recovery in sentiment during European trading takes some of the haven premium away from the dollar.

Verbal intervention warnings from Japanese Finance Minister Katayama and Chief Cabinet Secretary Kihara have also given traders an opportunity to fade some of the recent gains in USD/JPY, with the pair having roughly approached the prior rate-check zone above 158.

I remain cynical over how much today’s move will extend given the broader macro picture for Japan. While crude prices have pared gains today, their upward trajectory will likely weigh further on the post-election positivity as traders fret over the possibility of a more forceful fiscal stance from the government. Additionally, it raises the risk that the BOJ will struggle to tighten policy in the near-term due to the potential growth shock. Governor Ueda has already cautioned today that the central bank’s view will be different if the economy deviates from its outlook.

Whilst US equity futures are higher, today’s rally feels precarious. Should they falter, USD/JPY will likely return to this week’s trading pattern with the dollar the dominant haven. @parisprogram

這種快速的上升導致後備貨價差大幅加幅，當出現即時合約命令時，價格優惠顯示中東的產能正面临日益嚴峻的威脅，出口受到阻礙，例如伊拉克等產地開始關閉生產線。儘管總統特朗普已提供軍艦護航和保險保障以協助恢復油輪運輸，但運輸業界仍希望在這樣危險的環境中深入參與之前，能獲得更多確切的保證。
JPMorgan 指出，一些中東產油企業僅在幾週或甚至幾天內就將耗盡儲存被擱置（stranded）原油的能力。布倫特原油在週一/週二上漲了 12%，這是自 2020 年以來最大的單日漲幅。時間差利差（time-spreads）的劇烈上升表明，這波原油價格的上漲可能還遠未結束。三月結束時，布倫特原油的價格將會是多少？請在最新的市場脈動（Markets Pulse）調查中告訴我們。
🟢 While oil has pared some its gains, crude market spreads indicates the price rally may have much further to run.

A chart of the front-spread in Brent futures -- often used by traders as a proxy for supply tightness -- is a stark vertical line, having surged more than 500% this week. Such a rapid steepening of backwardation, when prompt contracts command a premium, reflects the escalating threat to Middle East output, as exports freeze up and producers like Iraq begin to shut-in production.
While President Trump has offered naval escorts and insurance guarantees to help get tanker traffic moving again, the shipping industry wants more assurances before plunging into such a hazardous environment. JPMorgan points out some Middle East producers are just weeks or even days away from exhausting their ability to store stranded barrels.

Brent jumped 12% on Monday/Tuesday, the biggest gain since 2020. The sharp run up in time-spreads suggests the surge may be far from over.

By contrast, Saudi Arabia and Israel’s indexes are rising as investors rotate toward companies perceived as relatively insulated from the immediate fallout. Saudi Arabia’s Al Rajhi Bank is up as elevated oil prices are viewed as supportive of the kingdom’s fiscal position and domestic liquidity conditions. In Tel Aviv, Elbit Systems is the largest positive contributor to the TA-35, tracking a broader global rally in defense contractors.

Local currencies present similar dispersion. The Egyptian pound has weakened past 50 per dollar in the offshore market, while the Israeli shekel is up and trades a smidgen away from a multiyear high.

These moves suggest investors are recalibrating risk exposure while awaiting clearer signals on the scope and duration of the conflict. Past episodes in the region suggest that once the geopolitical premium is priced, attention often shifts back to macro fundamentals — fiscal buffers, oil prices and external balances. If that pattern holds, the current volatility may well lead to cheaper entry points for investors focused on relative value rather than headline risk. @parisprogram

South Korea is both a critical bottleneck in the artificial intelligence supply chain and one of the world’s most externally energy-dependent economies. Higher energy costs therefore pose a downside risk to global AI stocks are already grappling with valuation concerns.

To be clear, this isn’t an attempt to explain why the Kospi Index just logged its largest daily fall on record. South Korean stocks have been leading global equity gains for some time so it is not a huge surprise that they are leading the selloff too.

But the implications of the conflict in Iran pose a risk that goes beyond those eye-catching moves.

South Korea imports nearly all of the oil and gas it consumes with an energy mix that’s still heavily reliant on fossil fuels, leaving it vulnerable to the surge in prices we’ve seen since the war began. That means South Korean firms that are key players in the AI supply chain also face the risk of higher input costs.

Natural gas for example accounted for around a quarter of South Korean electricity generation in 2024, according to IEA data. Longer term, those firms face the risk of second round effects as wages see upward pressure from higher energy bills.

Whether firms respond by curtailing production or hiking prices or both, the broader AI sector stands to suffer. The global industry is already facing a shortage of memory chips and South Korea, or more specifically SK Hynix and Samsung, account for roughly two-thirds of global DRAM and HBM market share, according to Counterpoint Research.

If that crunch worsens and filters through to profits, investors are likely to be unforgiving. There are already growing concerns over whether vast capital spending plans can ultimately generate adequate returns and justify current valuations. Higher energy costs could compound those fears. @parisprogram

Whether you are bullish or bearish on gold, as our team has debated over the past day, it is certainly not behaving like a reliable safe haven.

The conflict in Iran did trigger an initial shock response at the start of the week, and the overall market moves have been relatively orderly. But while rate, FX, equity and even oil volatility remain well below the levels seen during the tariff shock on Liberation Day, the notable exception is gold.One of the four key characteristics of a safe haven, as outlined in my Macro View, is that it should act as a store of value. Investors do not want to bleed capital, so haven assets should preserve purchasing power with low real volatility.

Gold is clearly not meeting that criterion. Volatility has shifted structurally higher as demand from investors seeking an everything hedge has poured into a relatively small market. That influx has created nervous price action at overbought extremes, amplified by the herding behaviour associated with retail participation.

A safe haven should also exhibit negative correlation with risk assets. Gold’s decline alongside equities yesterday does not fit that definition.

Whether gold ultimately rises or falls from here, it is fair to say it is not currently providing consistent portfolio protection. @parisprogram

⚠️ Even though inflation breakevens’ move has been muted, the rise in nominal yields still has a lot do with the uncertainty stoked by rising price pressures.

Ten-year Treasury yields are up around 15 bps this week, ostensibly on inflation fears, but breakevens are up only modestly. In fact, only about 20% of the net move is from a rise in inflation expectations, with the rest coming from real yields.
With oil prices around 15% higher since the US and Israel’s attacks on Iran, the rise in yields has been taken to be about inflation. That’s likely the case, but it doesn’t fit the breakdown above.

First, a rise in real yields isn’t always about an expected boost to real growth (which the Iran conflict would be unlikely to spur in the US).

Real yields can also climb when there is a rapid liquidation in long-duration positioning. I had noted there was an increase in short positioning in Treasuries in recent weeks, but it’s possible fast money/CTA funds got long at the end of last week and quickly stopped out (and perhaps reversed their positions) this week.

And real yields are also driven by uncertainty. Bond vol has picked up in recent days and this must be reflected in yields. It’s not currently being picked up in conventional measures of term premium, such as the ACM model. But a real-time version of 10-year term premium, based on the 2s5s10s yield fly and short the 30-year, has been rising.
Essentially, the rise in bond volatility captures the increase in inflation risk from its expected magnitude and its more uncertain path, as well as a more clouded outlook around the path of the short-term rate (complicated by a new Federal Reserve chair taking the helm soon). These are feeding into a rise in real yields.

It cannot be assumed, therefore, that the muted rise in breakevens is a sign that yields’ move has little to do with inflation. That’s because the risks from rising price growth also show up in the uncertainty that it causes. @parisprogram

美國股市持續表現堅強，但觀察到的基礎波動曲線顯示，目前尚未出現明顯的恐慌情緒。S&P 500 的看跌市場現象實際上更多是出於對未來價格上漲風險的降低，而非爲了防範指數進一步下跌。這從 VIX 指數所反映的數據中可以看得出，該指數主要反映的是價格上漲風險的減少，而非對指數深層下跌的擔憂。
相反，價格的下跌更多是將過度風險從市場中消除，而非真正降低風險。VIX指數的上升反映了這種更大的風險。我們可以建立一個有效的模型來描述這種現象，即虛值看跌期權與虛值看漲期權之間的權利金差異。
但這並非因風險厭惡導致的大幅上升的「put skew」所驅動，而是因「call skew」突然大幅下降所驅動。這更暗示了市場預期上漲時常見的風險賭注類型，而非預期有顯著的下挫。這種情況或許會有所改變。
但這會增加gamma為負值的概率，從而為股票價格的更大幅下跌提供動力。伊朗的衝突是否足以在三月結束前導致S&P 500指數下跌10%？請在最新的市場脈動調查中告訴我們。
Option markets for the S&P 500 show that the fall in price and the rise in risk, as captured by the VIX, have been more about reducing upside exposure than insuring against deeper downside in the index.

The US stock market continues to hold its ground remarkably well. But looking at the underlying vol surface shows there has yet to be much resembling panic setting in. Instead, the decline in prices has been more about taking excess risk off the table, rather than dialling it significantly down.

Reflecting the greater risks is the rise in the VIX. We can build a good model for it as the difference between out-of-the-money put and call skew. But rather than the increase being driven by a significant rise in put skew, as would be expected if risk aversion was extreme, the bulk has been driven by a sharp fall in call skew.
That’s more suggestive of punters taking excess risk off the table of the type typical when the market is expected to rise (as is the case most of the time), rather than expecting significant downside.

That may change of course. But it brings with it the higher likelihood that gamma goes negative, providing the sustenance for much sharper drops in stocks.

市場一旦看到伊朗戰爭緩衝區的解決方案變得具體化，就會出現大幅反轉，因為石油市場中因戰爭而積累的風險優勢是暫時的。僅僅是暗示存在緩衝區的跡象，比如《紐約時報》報導伊朗透過非公開渠道進行接觸（後來又被否認），似乎就足以拉低原油價格，而這正是風險情緒需要恢復的充分條件。
特朗普的政策在周二正式實施，其目的是通過支持霍爾穆茲地區的油輪運輸來減緩能源衝擊的影響。這雖然降低了能源衝擊的長期風險，但同時也突顯了戰艦參與其中時可能帶來的巨大風險。該政策能夠迅速推進，是因為長期的通貨膨脹預測幾乎沒有發生變化。
五年期的報酬率約為 2.5%，而五年的五年期前測值低於 2.2%，這並不代表市場正確地評估了由石油價格波動所引發的持續性通貨膨脹問題。根據最近的標準來看，金融狀況也相對寬鬆；芝加哥聯邦儲備銀行的 NFCI 指數在最新數據中仍為負值，為 -0.57。因此，標普 500 指數（SPX）一直維持着其原有的波動範圍，無論遇到多大的宏觀經濟衝擊都沒有破壞這個範圍，而同時企業的領導層也在進行更換。
伊朗衝擊的影響十分明顯，而這週的市場動態也波及了人工智能（AI）產業；記憶技術相關的交易表現不佳，儘管根本的供應短缺問題依然存在。周三早盤的價格走勢顯示，有投資者對這些相關標的進行了買入操作。市場的錯誤在於將地緣政治因素帶來的優勢視為持續不變的，然而在這種快速變化的環境中，相關的市場規範和說法很快就会過時。
隨著市場定位越來越負面，且潛在風險也隨之增加，標準普爾500指數（S&P 500）近期在價格區間內出現「價格壓縮」（price squeeze）的風險也日益增長，目前指數價格約在7,000點左右。
Markets are set up for a sharp reversal the moment an Iran conflict off-ramp turns tangible, because the war premium sitting in oil is fleeting given fundamentals. Hints of off-ramps alone, such as the New York Times report that Iran reached out via back channels (since denied), are seemingly enough to knock crude lower, and that’s all risk sentiment needs to heal.

The Trump put kicked in Tuesday with the administration pledge to backstop tanker traffic through Hormuz. That reduces the odds of a lasting energy shock, even if it also underlines how fat the tail risks are when warships become part of the plumbing.

The reason the tape can move on so quickly is that longer-run inflation pricing is barely flinching. Five-year breakevens are around 2.5%, and the five-year, five-year forward measure sitting under 2.2%, isn’t exactly a market pricing a persistent oil-driven inflation problem. Financial conditions also look loose by recent standards, with the Chicago Fed’s NFCI still negative at -0.57 in its latest reading.
That leaves the SPX doing what it has done for months, absorbing macro punches without breaking its range, while leadership gets reshuffled. The Iran shock has been loud, yet this week’s factor moves also caught the AI complex, with the memory trade underperforming even as the underlying shortage theme hasn’t vanished. Wednesday’s early price action shows dip buying in those names.

The market’s mistake risk is treating a geopolitical premium as sticky, when this is a headline regime where narratives expire fast. With positioning as negative as it is and implied vol this elevated, the right tail risk is growing for a squeeze back to the top of the S&P 500’s recent range at 7,000. @parisprogram

後期ISM指數的上升推動標普500指數稍微接近了其2026年波動範圍的中點，這其實並不難理解。以收盤價計算，目前SPX的波動範圍不到2.7%。這不僅是今年至今SPX波動範圍最狹小的情況，也比自1896年以來的杜萬工業指數的所有波動範圍都要小。
這一事發生在 a) 伊朗衝突的背景下，以及 b) 其他地方處於極度波動之中，實在令人費解，至少說是令人困惑。對此，當然存在一些爭議，有人認為這代表極端的自滿，也有人認為這顯示對伊朗衝突將會迅速解決的信心。支持後者的觀點需要說明，為何預測市場僅將和平談判在月底前的機率估計為略高於三分之一。
今日的財經觀察專欄與播客討論了這種令人困惑的設定，並強調了在對未來發展進行預測或推測時，加入置信區間的重要性。
The post-ISM bump has pushed the S&P 500 a bit closer to the midpoint of its 2026 range, which admittedly doesn’t take much work. After all, on a closing-price basis the SPX range thus far is less than 2.7%. Not only is that the tightest trading range at this point in the year in the history of the SPX, but it’s also tighter than any range in the Dow Industrials going all the way back to 1896.
That this has happened a) amidst the backdrop of the Iran conflict and b) with extreme volatility elsewhere is bemusing, to say the least. There is certainly some debate over whether this represents extreme complacency or confidence that the Iran conflict will be swiftly resolved. Those arguing in favor of the latter need to explain why prediction markets only ascribe a bit more than a one-in-three chance of a ceasefire by month end.

Today’s Macro Man column and podcast discuss this bemusing (or is that confusing?) set-up and the importance of putting a confidence interval around any forecast or projection of how things will play out. @parisprogram

自特朗普就任總統以來，日圓、瑞士法郎、美國債券及黃金都成為日常股價損失的更佳緩衝，相比曾經統治市場的美元。當然，風險資產與日圓之間的負相關關係只是避險資產的一個方面，目前環境中與石油相關的挑戰使依賴進口的貨幣，如日圓，變得更加脆弱。
儘管如此，這仍突顯了美元地位所遭受的更廣泛損害。如前所述，政策風險將持續削弱由中東衝突所引發的通貨膨脹動機所帶來的積極前景。尤其是在財政部長斯科特·貝斯頓提醒投資人關稅將恢復到原來的稅率之後，儘管最高法院已推翻了相關判決。

Despite renewed optimism about the dollar’s haven appeal, the currency has severely dropped on the list of effective hedges in a Trump regime fraught with policy risk.

That share is still higher than in the five years prior, however. A greater observation is how much the dollar has dropped on the haven list. Since President Donald Trump took office, the Japanese yen, the Swiss franc, Treasuries and bullion all served as a better buffer for daily equity losses than the greenback, which once dominated. Of course, a negative correlation with risk assets is only one of the facets that define a haven, and oil-related challenges in the current environment leave import-dependent currencies, such as the yen, vulnerable.

Nonetheless, it underscores the damage done to the dollar’s status more broadly. As noted previously, policy risk will continue to undermine the more constructive outlook driven by the inflationary impulse tied to the Middle East conflict. Not least after Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent reminded investors that tariffs will go back to their old rate, despite the Supreme Court’s overturn.

1973年的阿拉伯-以色列戰爭，亦稱為逾越節戰爭，與當前與伊朗的衝突有許多相似之處。當時的市場結果並不理想：石油價格暴漲了四倍，美元升值超過15%，股票市場經歷了自大蕭條以來最嚴重的崩盤。
當日，通膨在能源衝擊來臨時已經升高，貨幣政策過於鬆散，聯邦儲備局的獨立性也受到質疑；而股市在Nifty Fifty時代則僅由少數幾家公司所主導。如果歷史重演，最謹慎的投資組合將從這種「尾風風險」中受益。更多內容請參見周三的MacroScope報告：《猶太曆逾越節戰爭對股市的嚴峻預示》。
The 1973 Arab-Israeli War, also known as the Yom Kippur War, has several similarities to today’s conflict with Iran. The market outcome was not good: oil quadrupled, the dollar rallied over 15% and stocks faced their worst bear market since the Great Depression.
Then as today, inflation was already elevated when an energy shock hit, monetary policy was inappropriately loose and the Federal Reserve’s independence was in question, while the stock market, in the era of the Nifty Fifty, was driven by only a handful of names. If history rhymes, the most prudent portfolios will benefit from tail-risk insurance.

AI 交易正處於另一個關鍵的營收考驗階段，而目前的宏觀經濟環境對其並不有利。Nvidia 上週取得了出色的業績，但其股價仍不斷下跌，這顯示了科技企業想要獲得回報的門檻已經抬高至極高水平。由於對企業估值以及超級運算器在 AI 領域投資的持續性擔憂，投資者已經開始轉向其他投資對象。
現在，地緣政治緊張關係的不斷升級為本已承受壓力的股權市場增添了新的風險避險因素。在這樣不穩定的環境中，單純依靠強大的基本面可能已不足以應對市場挑戰。因此，博通在收市後公布的業績報告將不僅僅是一份公司簡單的財務數據更新。分析師預計，該公司在財年第一季的每股收益約為 2.03 美元，營收將接近 193 億美元，其中與人工智能（AI）相關的銷售額將增長近一倍，達到約 82 億美元。
股票價格相比去年12月的最高點下跌了約22%，現在的交易價格約為其現金流量的27倍，遠低於高點時所達到的42倍倍數。由於投資者關注人工智能（AI）領域的後續發展、超級運營商的需求以及利潤率的走勢，博通（Broadcom）不僅需要超額完成預測，還可能需要進一步提升市場對AI基礎設施支出下一階段的信心。
否則，另一種「好並不夠好」的市場反應會加強這種觀感：即目前這個周期的特點在於多方面的壓縮，而非企業盈利的改善。
The AI trade is heading into another key earnings test at a time when the macro backdrop is doing it no favors.

Nvidia delivered blockbuster results last week and still saw its shares slide, underscoring how high the bar has become for tech to be rewarded. Investors are already rotating away from the largest names amid concerns about valuation and the durability of hyperscaler AI spending. Now, escalating geopolitical tensions are adding a fresh layer of risk aversion to an equity market that was already under pressure. In this shaky environment, strong fundamentals alone may not be enough.

That sets up Broadcom’s results after the close as more than just a company update. Analysts expect fiscal 1Q EPS of about $2.03 on revenue near $19.3 billion, with AI-related sales nearly doubling to roughly $8.2 billion. The stock is down about 22% from its December peak and trades around 27 times forward earnings — well off the 42x multiple it commanded at the highs.

With investors focused on AI backlog growth, hyperscaler demand and margin trajectory, Broadcom has to do more than clear estimates; it likely needs to reinforce confidence in the next leg of AI infrastructure spending. Otherwise, another good-isn’t-good-enough reaction would deepen the sense that this phase of the cycle is about multiple compression, not earnings beats. @parisprogram

市場中主動的美國債券殖利率差價在周三穩定下來，交易員考慮到與伊朗的戰爭時間線縮短，使油價趨穩。隨著對聯邦儲備系統因油價上漲而採取強硬政策的預期減弱，近期的策略性縮小利差可能放緩。這一直以來都是與伊朗衝突的經濟影響依其持續時間而定。
隨著原油價格的上升，2年期與10年期債券的利差收窄。這是因為交易員預期通貨膨脹會推動原油價格繼續上漲，並且認為戰爭的持續時間會比最初預計的更長。
曲線迅速擴張，隨著原油獲利反轉後，紐約時報報導伊朗情報部門透過另一國間諜機構與中央情報局接觸，提出談判條件以結束衝突，但隨著早盤交易中部份回撤。
雖然該報告後來被否認，但其中暗示即便伊朗已轉向長期戰爭治理，局勢仍有可能緩和。這使得原油價格在本周上漲超過10%後有所回穩，減緩了近期通膨對市場的影響；這種影響也體現在前端收益率上，同時收益率曲線因此發生變化（向後移動），因為消費需求受到較少的影響。
此外，周三的ISM服務數據呈現出理想的狀態——數據水平達到2022年夏天以來的最高值，雖然支付價格低於預期，但新訂單和就業數據卻大幅上升，Cameron Crise如此評述。
雖然曲線平滑的趨勢可能還會持續一段時間，但根據韋納·拉姆的觀點，如果聯邦儲備系統（Fed）對油價上漲和伊朗衝突採取過度緊縮的應對措施，那麼出現「機會性陡升器」（opportunistic steepener）的機會就會增加。聯邦儲備系統理事長史蒂芬·米蘭認為，目前對伊朗衝突的影響還難以準確評估，因此現在制定政策還為時过早，但他仍然認為聯邦儲備系統有降息的空間。在伊朗衝突的形勢尚不確定的情況下，聯邦儲備系統仍會採取戰術性的措施來平滑利率曲線。
然而，任何表明衝突會更快發生的跡象都會引發強烈的反對，並向那些持有大量頭寸的交易者發出重新進入市場、參與指數波動交易的信號。實際上，我們在本週早些時候就觀察到了這一現象——當時市場對沖基金普遍開始擴大他們的投資組合中的指數波動幅度，這既降低了負面政策反應的可能性，也縮短了衝突的持續時間。根據麥可·巴爾（Michael Ball）的說法，這一理論在股市中也越來越受到認可。

Spreads between active Treasuries stabilized Wednesday as traders consider a shorter timeline for the war with Iran that has steadied oil. With expectations for a hawkish Federal Reserve response to oil spikes dimmed, recent tactical flattening can slow.

It was always the case that the economic impact of the conflict with Iran hinged on its duration. As crude oil rose, it flattened the benchmark 2s10s spread as traders priced in an inflationary impulse to rises in crude and expectations the conflict would last longer than initially expected.
Curves were quick to widen in response to a reversal of gains in crude oil after the New York Times reported operatives from Iran’s Ministry of Intelligence reached out to the CIA through another country’s spy agency with an offer to discuss terms for ending the conflict, but gave back some of that move in early afternoon trading. While the report was later denied, it suggested a potential de-escalation even amid reports Iran has shifted toward governing for a prolonged war. That steadied crude oil after a more than 10% gain this week -- stalling the near-term inflation impact reflected in front-end yields and shifts it out the the curve as consumption can be less affected.

What’s more, Wednesday’s ISM services report looked like Goldilocks data, as the headline reading posted its highest level since the summer of 2022, with lower-than-expected prices paid accompanied by nice jumps in new orders and employment, Cameron Crise wrote.

While curve flattening may have room to run as per Ven Ram, there are signs of opportunistic steepener initiation under the premise that a hawkish Fed response to oil spikes and the Iran conflict may be misplaced. Fed Governor Stephen Miran earlier said while it’s too early to shape policy views given the conflict in Iran, he still see scope to cut rates.

Tactical flatteners remain in play with the conflict in Iran fluid. However, any signs of a swifter conflict will elicit prompt pushback and a signal to macro traders to re-engage on steepeners. In fact, we saw this earlier in the week with macro hedge funds broadly initiated spread wideners Monday -- fading both the potential for a negative policy response and the duration of the conflict. This theory is gaining traction in stocks too, according to Michael Ball.

Will the yield curve steepen or flatten over the next month amid the war in Iran? Let us know in the latest Markets Pulse survey. @parisprogram

市場依然堅信聯邦儲備系統（Federal Reserve）今年會再次降息。然而，勞動市場的數據卻越來越對這一預期構成挑戰。Apollo的Torsten Slok指出，除了今天早間公布的二月份ADP數據強於預期、表明私人部門的招聘活動持續復甦之外，那些改換工作的人的薪資增長也開始加速。
如圖所示，此類別在後疫情復甦期間推動了薪資成長的浪潮。隨著薪資水平逐步回落，職業轉換者的薪資如今再次攀升。1月，該群體錄得自2022年11月以來最大月增，顯示勞動市場正重獲強勁的復甦力。
Slok 指出了幾個支持這一趨勢的動力，包括更嚴格的移民政策、由人工智能（AI）的廣泛應用所推動的經濟增長，以及唐納德·特朗普總統提出的「One Big Beautiful Bill」所帶來的財政刺激措施。現在最重要的是時機的選擇：這種轉變發生在通貨膨脹風險重新出現的背景下，同時經濟增長正在復甦，而中東地區的石油供應中斷也給經濟前景帶來了壓力。
早前今日，ISM市場服務價格指數跌至三月份以來的最低水平，就在此之後，雙邊關稅措施被宣布。然而，製造業指數卻顯示出相反的趨勢。再加上上週一的美國製造業生產者價格指數（PPI）報告所顯示的強勁傳導效應，通貨膨脹的警訊開始逐漸顯現。市場價格已經開始反映這種變化，交易員對年底前可能會有兩次降息的預測也產生了動搖。
在當前環境下，這種謹慎的態度顯得十分合理；如果能源價格持續居高不下，很可能會促使進一步的調整。明尼阿波利斯聯邦儲備銀行（Minneapolis Fed）的主席尼爾·卡什卡里（Neel Kashkari）早前也暗示過這一點。
The market remains adamant that the Federal Reserve will cut rates more this year. But labor-market indicators are increasingly challenging that outlook.

Beyond this morning’s stronger-than-expected ADP report for February — which pointed to a continued recovery in private hiring — wage growth among job switchers is also beginning to accelerate again, Apollo’s Torsten Slok notes.
As the chart shows, this category led the surge in wage growth during the post-pandemic recovery. After eventually converging with the rest on the way down, wages for job switchers are now breaking higher once more. In January, the group posted its largest monthly increase since November 2022 — a sign of renewed resilience in the labor market.

Slok points to several supportive forces behind the trend, including tighter immigration, economic growth driven by the AI boom and fiscal stimulus from President Donald Trump’s “One Big Beautiful Bill.” What matters now is the timing: The shift comes as inflation risks are re-emerging, with growth rebounding and Middle East oil disruptions adding pressure to the outlook.

Earlier today, the ISM services prices gauge printed at its lowest level since March, before reciprocal tariffs were announced. But the manufacturing equivalent told the opposite story. Combined with stronger pass-through suggested by last week’s January PPI report, inflation warning signs are starting to accumulate.
Market pricing has begun to reflect that shift, with traders now wavering on the prospect of two rate cuts by year-end. That caution looks reasonable in the current environment and could prompt further recalibration if energy prices remain elevated — something Minneapolis Fed President Neel Kashkari also hinted at earlier. @parisprogram

雖然今日服務調查的成長訊息較為樂觀，但下午的《Beige Book》報告內容則略顯混雜。誠然，聯準會的個別調查在大後方疫情後的時期普遍表現較為低迷，今日的報告則將成長描述為「微弱至中度」，遍及大多數地區。
雖然這似乎比一月份所給的「小幅到中等」的描述有所提升，但同時也導致報告中報告的地區數量從四個增加到五個。這正是所謂的「K 形態擴張」的體現！根據慣例，這份報告對資產價格並無明顯影響。消費者活動普遍被視為疲軟，不過這部分也部分歸因於天氣因素。
製造業有所改善，數據中心被視為需求的來源。勞動市場被認為是穩定的（這與近期數據相符），值得注意的是，人工智能（AI）被提及為提升員工生產力的方式，而非解雇他們的原因。價格上漲被視為較為平穩，但最近的石油價格變動可能會影響這一判斷。
就整體而言，雖然地區對未來似乎持較為樂觀的看法，這在一定程度上支持了經濟狀況良好（儘管未必需要降息）的觀點。
While the growth messaging from today’s services survey was quite upbeat, the takeaway from this afternoon’s Beige Book is a little more mixed. In fairness, the Fed’s anecdotal survey has been generally downbeat for most of the post-Covid era, and today’s edition characterized growth as “slight to moderate” across most districts. While that seems like an upgrade from the “slight to modest” description in January, by the same token the number of districts reporting stagnant or negative growth increased from four to five. It’s the K-shaped expansion writ large! Per the usual, the report has left little mark on asset prices.

Consumer activity was generally seen as subdued, albeit partially because of the weather. Manufacturing improved, with data centers cited as a source of demand. Labor markets were seen as stable (which generally corroborates recent data), though interestingly AI was cited as a way to enhance the productivity of workers rather than a reason for sacking them. Price increases were deemed as moderate, though of course recent oil price developments may alter that view. On balance, though, districts seem a little more optimistic about the future, so in a sense that does support the notion that the economy is doing just fine (though not necessarily that it needs rate cuts!) @parisprogram

加拿大銀行從暫時性的救濟措施轉向更結構化的解決方案，以應對Repo市場的緊張狀況，這將有助於緩和該國貨幣市場的壓力，並從長期角度支持市場的風險承受能力。近期，CORRA的成交價格曾一度高於加拿大銀行的隔夜利率，這顯示獲得安全資金的成本超出了政策所設定的範圍。
季末和年度結賬時的資產負債表限制不斷導致可預見的壓力點，因為對融資和高品質擔保品的需求激增。這個問題的根源對任何關注過去年美國聯邦儲備系統（Fed）在「量化緊縮」（QT）政策下耗盡儲備金、從而引發融資摩擦的人來說都應該很熟悉。當英國央行（BoC）縮減其資產負債表時，結算餘額下降，金融系統對需求突然增加的敏感度也隨之提高。
即使在資產回流至聯邦準備委員會（Fed）預估的穩定狀態範圍之後，壓力依然存在，因為現金並未得到均勻分配。大銀行的資產集中度形成了一種負面影響，降低了準備金在金融系統中的流動速度。再加上債券和期貨交易中的不確定性，以及結算方式的改變（從T+0結算改为T+1結算），這些因素共同導致CORRA指數在最關鍵的時刻持續上升，遠離預定目標。
加拿大央行已將存款利率降至逾夜利率5個基點以下，以促進資金流向央行，減少資金在央行的積聚。當需要時，該機構也重新啟動了Repo操作，包括在壓力恢復後進行資產注入。QT（貨幣市場融資）已結束，市場將回到常規購入，以保持結算餘額穩定。根據RBC的Simon Deeley所言，若利差維持穩定，即將來到貨幣購入的時間。
更大的轉變在於加拿大央行（BoC）的舉動：該央行計畫在2027年早期加入CCMS（中央清算系統）以實現repo（回購協議）的中央清算，接著再加入CDCC（中央清算公司）來進行自身的repo操作。中央清算的重要性在於它能提升淨對沖效果、減少資產負債表的使用，并在壓力時期提升repo交易的可靠性。同時，這也有助於建立長期的repo市場，而不仅仅是短期的交易。
此舉對市場結構產生顯著影響。若 CORRA 停止過度超前，聯邦儲備系統（聯準會）可減少政策執行的摩擦，市場對金融條件的影響也會減輕。
The Bank of Canada’s shift from temporary relief to a more structural fix for repo tightness will ease pressure in the country’s money markets and support risk sentiment long term.

CORRA has at times recently traded above the Bank of Canada’s overnight rate — a sign secured funding costs are running hotter than the policy corridor is meant to deliver. Quarter-end and year-end balance-sheet constraints keep creating predictable pinch points as demand for funding and high-quality collateral jumps.

The root cause will sound familiar to anyone who watched US funding frictions flare as QT drained reserves at the Fed last year. As the BoC shrank its balance sheet, settlement balances fell and the system became more sensitive to spikes in demand. Even after balances moved back into the BoC’s estimated steady-state range, the stress kept showing up because the cash isn’t evenly held. Concentration at the biggest banks creates a dynamic that slows the velocity of reserves through the system. Add noise from bond and futures positioning, plus the move to T+1 settlement, and you get CORRA drifting above target at the worst possible moments.
The BoC has already cut the deposit rate to 5 bps below the overnight rate to discourage parking cash at the central bank and push more money into repo. It has also restarted repo operations when needed, including injection after strains resurfaced. QT has ended, with a return to regular purchases to keep settlement balances stable. Bill purchases may arrive sooner than previously thought if spreads stay sticky, according to RBC’s Simon Deeley.

The bigger shift is the BoC’s move toward centrally cleared repo by joining CCMS by early 2027 and then joining CDCC to centrally clear its own repo operations, according to reporting by colleague Erik Hertzberg. Central clearing matters because it boosts netting, reduces balance-sheet usage, and makes repo access more reliable during periods of stress. It also helps build out a term repo market, not just overnight.

This move has an outsized confidence effect on the market plumbing. If CORRA stops overshooting, the BoC can steer policy with less friction — and markets get less of a weight on financial conditions from plumbing. @parisprogram

💥 週三股市上揚，油價也保持不變，看起來伊朗周圍的恐慌已經減退。不過風險仍存，尤其對私人信貸市場而言，這類市場規模如此龐大，甚至可能失去專業化帶來的更高報酬。現在，去年我特別分析了事件風險對股票的影響。
我的分析顯示，過去三十年來，所謂的黑天鹅風險在所有情況下都導致了短期衝擊，除了2008年。因此，最大的風險是，即使伊朗戰爭無法持續長時間對經濟造成顯著損害，它仍可能足夠暴露現有的脆弱性，這些脆弱性同時影響資產價格和更廣泛的信貸環境。這是否構成了所謂的「完美风暴」？
高通膨與殖利率上漲對私人信貸產業的影響尚未被完全理解，相關規範也不完善，風險也未得到有效控制。請閱讀此專欄：《私人信貸的風險裂痕風險正在加深：所有風險》。
💥 With stocks rallying on Wednesday and oil prices mostly unchanged, it looks like the panic around Iran has subsided. Still, risks remain, especially for private credit, a market whose size is so great it arguably has lost the specialization that warrants higher returns.

Now, last year I looked at the impact of event risk on stocks in particular. My analysis showed that in all cases of so-called Black Swan risk over the last three decades shocks were short-lived except in 2008. So the biggest risk is that, even if the war in Iran doesn’t last long enough to meaningfully hurt the economy, it could still be enough to expose existing vulnerabilities that weigh on both asset prices and the wider credit environment.

The perfect storm? Knock-on effects of higher inflation and rising yields in an industry - private credit - that isn’t yet fully understood, regulated and properly guarded against risk.

The high-volatility oil-shock seen in recent days is showing signs of slowing. That’s drawing in buying in mortgage-backed securities — a sign that support for key corners of US fixed income has started to form.

Crude oil has rallied more than 10% this week as the US-Iran conflict effectively closed shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, a choke point for about 20% of global oil exports. Those gains slowed Wednesday -- WTI futures were recently up about 1.9% -- calming inflationary pressures and capping yield rises.
There are signs volatility is calming too. Market turbulence as measured by 2-year/10-year swaptions, a key measure for mortgage volatility, fell Wednesday, after rising in the prior four days. That’s good news after the MOVE Index, a gauge of bond market volatility, rose to its highest since November as of Tuesday’s close.

Because mortgages have optionality related to prepayments, that fall in vol ushered in cautious buying for the first time since the war started, according to dealer desks that see the flow. That’s logical since rises in volatility and headline risk can take a while to digest and thus spark investment. If vol continues to subside, more demand for MBS will likely continue to materialize.

What’s more, a slowing in yield increases has lowered the shock of home-loan origination, taking supply pressures off MBS.

MBS also gain support as the Fannie Mae 30-year current-coupon versus a 5/10-year Treasury blend tightened for the first day in eight — signaling increased demand for MBS over Treasuries.

And mortgages are holding their weightings for the first time this week. That means the securities are maintaining their relative percentage within fixed-income portfolios and aren’t falling in value compared with other assets like Treasuries. @parisprogram

以下為投資者將關注的主題，來自政府的工作報告、財政預算及草案第十五個五年計畫：

成長目標：布魯金斯學院經濟學部預測，最有可能的結果是國內生產總值（GDP）成長目標在4.5%至5.0%之間。
政策優先次序：工作報告與五年計畫中使用的語言與順序——特別是關於消費支持強度的訊號、財政補貼的實際規模與目標，或額外措施以協助現有房屋購置。缺乏具體細節可能使股權資產暴露於地緣政治與關稅等外部風暴之中。
在地方政府的五年規劃中，技術與人工智能（AI）已經成為重點發展領域。這些計劃明確提出了對人工智能及相关產業的扶持措施。中央政府政策的具體內容將對投資者判斷科技股的高估值具有決定性影響。去年，財政赤字佔GDP的4%，分析師普遍預計今年的財政政策將維持在相似的水平。
一個大幅增加的財政赤字或超出預期的政府債券發行量，將表明政府將採取更積極的財政刺激措施——這對股市有利，但可能對債券市場造成負面影響。在人民幣國際化方面：投資者將關注跨境支付系統的進展、資本賬戶開放的情況，以及任何能支持離岸人民幣融資和流動性的舉措。
這些措施可能有助於減少對美元的依賴，降低離岸融資成本，並提升以人民幣計價資產的吸引力，從而為貨幣提供更長遠的支持。

Growth target: Bloomberg Economics sees the most likely outcome as a GDP growth target in the 4.5%–5.0% range.
Policy priorities: The language and the ordering used in the work report and Five-Year Plan — particularly any signals about the strength of support for consumption, the true scale and targeting of fiscal subsidies, or additional measures to aid the acquisition of existing homes. Technology and AI: Many local governments already highlighted artificial intelligence and related industries in their Five-Year Plan proposals. Yuan internationalization: Investors will be watching for progress on cross-border payment systems, capital-account opening and anything to support offshore yuan financing and liquidity. 💥 Risk assets look to be shaking off some of the drag of the Iran conflict, with the exception of Treasuries that continue to be haunted by inflation risks.

The selloff that began Monday turned into an all-out rout on Tuesday, with previously high-flying South Korean equities suffering the worst of the losses. That looks to be over now as the rally in risk assets is wide-ranging from the S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 to Bitcoin. Gold and silver were up on Wednesday too after ceding ground the day prior.

That price action makes sense, as Black Swan risks over the last three decades indicate the initial negative market reaction to unexpected events was almost never sustained over a one-year time horizon. Only in 2008 did volatility from event risk turn pear-shaped for the long haul.

The only asset class in trouble seems to be US Treasuries, which lost ground for a third day. The shorter end of the curve led declines on shrinking rate cut bets, with about 40 bps to 41 bps priced in for 2026. As recently as late February, that number was 60 bps. Core PCE inflation through December was already 3.0%, and now prices face additional upward pressure from tax cuts, 15% tariffs and an oil surge.

Yet for markets beyond bonds, the picture is brighter. Even a mixed Fed Beige Book showed Fed districts a bit more optimistic about the future, while Wednesday’s services ISM was an out-and-out Goldilocks print.

市場對週四的30年國債發售時機感到難以預料，因為中東戰爭的升級讓日本的通貨膨脹前景變得更加樂觀。然而，這也使得日本銀行總局無計可施，它必須通過提前提高利率來應對這種影響。在這樣不確定的環境下，任何主要的中央銀行都不會考慮提高利率，因此債券市場的投資者將會自行採取行動來打壓長期債券的價格。
這將導致與日元的負面反饋循環，因為在美國債券收益率持續上升的壓力下，日元勢必會繼續承受壓力。本週美國30年期國庫券的收益率上升了約1個基點，這將對日本國債（JGB）造成負面影響。所有這些因素都表明在今天的公開招標中需求十分疲弱，競標與成交比率有可能首次跌破3.0，這是自去年6月以來首次出現的情況。
這場低迷展望的唯一對抗，或許是日本生命保險公司必須進行嚴格的強制施壓，以促使其積極購入資產。然而，隨著財政年度僅剩幾週，他們已經累積了大量市場價值認列的債券損失。這恐怕太過苛刻了。
The timing for Thursday’s 30-year JGB sale could hardly be more challenging as the conflict in the Middle East increases the prospect of higher Japanese inflation, but ties the hands of the Bank of Japan to counter the impact with an early interest-rate hike.

No major central bank would consider raising rates in such an uncertain environment, which means that bond vigilantes will take it upon themselves to bash long-term bonds. That will open up a negative feedback loop with the yen, which is set to stay under pressure amid firm US yields. Treasury 30-year yields are around 12bps higher this week which will weigh on JGBs.

All of which points to low demand at today’s auction, with the prospect of the bid-to-cover ratio plunging below 3.0 for the first time since last June. The only counter to this downbeat outlook would be some serious arm twisting of Japan’s life insurance companies to step up and buy. However, with the fiscal year-end just a few weeks away and they are already sitting on large mark-to-market bond losses. That is probably just too much to ask for. @parisprogram

今年年初，銀價與韓國Kospi指數同步上漲，吸引了跨資產與零售投資者的關注，這顯示韓國股市的復甦勢頭難以持續太久。与此同时，這種積極的市場氛圍也傳到了日本，Topix與日經225指數也隨之上升。
South Korea’s stocks are leaping in early Thursday business as expected given the rebound for futures in the overnight session, but the path ahead won’t be smooth if silver is a guide. Especially with the end of the first quarter just over three weeks away and macro traders going through a period of asset whiplash.

Indeed, the early jump is so big that a program trading halt is initiated within the opening minutes.

Earlier this year silver and the Kospi were climbing hand-in-hand with cross-asset and retail investors involved in both sides, which suggests the rebound for Korean stocks won’t sustain for long.

Meanwhile, the initial positive vibes are spreading to Japan where the Topix and Nikkei are also higher. @parisprogram

日本股市周四的反彈可能還會持續——其中一個原因可能是短線投資者的過度佈局，這增加了短線資金被逼平（short squeeze）的風險。Topix指數連續三個交易日下跌，而東京證券交易所的短線賣壓已經升至43%——這一比例在過去一年中經常出現在股市的短期底部時。
短期資產負債（shorting）在鋼鐵、零售、銀行、電子產品和運輸等週期性且利率敏感的領域表現尤為突出，這些領域通常在全球情緒改善時快速回升。鋼鐵股價單獨看，短期資產負債已超過總交易量的半數。這並不代表宏觀風險已消失。
高能源成本及中東地緣政治不確定性仍對日本股市前景造成負面影響，但近期股價持續下跌、日圓貶值以及石油價格上升等多重負面因素，可能會促使市場進入一段調整期。尤其是當美元走弱、日圓價格穩定後，這些因素有望為股市帶來喘息之機。
Japanese equities’ rebound Thursday may have further to run -- one reason may be stretched positioning which raises the risk of a short squeeze.

The Topix had fallen for three consecutive sessions just as short selling on the Tokyo Stock Exchange climbed to 43% — a level that over the past year has often coincided with near-term bottoms.

Shorting has been especially elevated in steel, retail, banks, electronics and transportation — cyclical and rate-sensitive segments that tend to rebound quickly when global sentiment improves. Steel stocks alone saw short selling exceed half of total trading volume.
That’s not to say macro risks have disappeared. Higher energy costs and geopolitical uncertainty around the Middle East still cloud the outlook for Japanese stock market, but the recent triple whammy of weaker equities, a softer yen and rising oil prices may be due for a pause, especially as the US dollar eases and the yen finds some footing. @parisprogram

今日美元日元的交割匯率為 156.60，數據來自 MUFG。這個數據出人意料地偏低，顯示日本企業正在積極在 3 月 31 日財政年終前賣出美元。這種動態未來幾周將會產生更重要的影響，而市場似乎尚未完全將其考慮在內。實際上，這可能會打消日元重新成為資金貨幣的預期。
即使在中東危機可能導致日本央行加息延期的情況下，更大的企業資金流仍然為日本政府提供了支持，這可能意味着沒有必要急於進行外幣干預。
Today’s USD/JPY fixing at 156.60 from MUFG is surprisingly low and suggests that Japanese companies are lining up to sell dollars ahead of the fiscal year end on March 31.

That’s a dynamic which is set to play a bigger role in the weeks ahead and is unlikely to have been fully priced into markets. Indeed, it may quash notions of the yen making a return as a funding currency. Even with a likely delay to any Bank of Japan rate hike amid the Middle East crisis.

Moreover, bigger corporate flows give Japanese authorities a helping hand and may mean there’s no need for any rush into FX intervention. @parisprogram

亞洲市場貨幣波動本周升至發展中國家市場之上，這是自2025年5月以來首次出現。亞洲貨幣市場相對平靜的紀錄期結束，顯示出穩定原油價格對這些經濟體的重要性。亞洲貨幣價格波動的未來是否仍高於發展中國家市場，將在很大程度上取決於能源價格是否持續高位。
在美國和以色列攻擊伊朗之前，投資者已大量流入新興市場（EM）的資產——包括股票、債券和貨幣——這導致原油價格暴漲。隨之而來的是新興市場貨幣的暴跌，尤其是那些淨進口石油的國家的貨幣。從韓元在周二的惡劣賣出情況可以看出，某些地區出現了風險價值（VaR）衝擊，這是自2009年以來最嚴重的終盤賣出行情。
有潛在的 EM 市場波動趨勢將緩和——考慮到通膨從低基底開始上行，中央銀行持有的外幣資產較為豐厚，以及全球成長前景強勁。但近來中東地區的不確定性將使短期價格波動維持在較為極端的範圍。
JPMorgan的EM波動指數在209天連續期間均低於同組七國的指數，持續時間直至上週一，這是自2000年以來最長的紀錄。
Emerging-market currency volatility surged above developed-market peers this week for the first time since May 2025. The end of a record stretch of relative calm for EM FX underscores how vital it is for such economies that crude prices stabilize.

Whether expectations for EM FX price swings remain above DM peers will largely depend on how elevated energy prices remain. Investors had been piling into EM assets —- equities, bonds, currencies — before the US and Israel attacked Iran and sent crude prices soaring. That sent EM currencies tumbling, especially those from net oil-importing economies. There are signs of a VaR shock in some pockets, as seen from the nasty selloff in the won on Tuesday, the worst selldown on a closing basis since 2009.

There’s potential for EM volatility to moderate — given inflation starting off from a low base, hefty central bank foreign reserves and a robust global growth outlook. But the near-term uncertainty over the Middle East will keep price swings at the wilder end for the short term.

JPMorgan’s EM volatility index was below a similar Group-of-Seven gauge for 209 consecutive days up until Monday — the longest stretch on record in data going back to 2000. @parisprogram

他們在市場劇烈下跌的背景下以約9.57億美元購入了這些股份，當時基準指數Kospi下跌了12%，這是該指數自1980年創立以來最大幅度的跌幅。海外投資者以前所未有的速度在Kosdaq市場購買小型公司的股票。週三的市場崩盤可能已經重新調整了股價，為海外投資者提供了尋找買入機會的機會。
周四開市時，Kospi 指數累計上漲 12%，這顯示國內投資者可能也加入了這波上漲的行列。許多韓國零售投資者將李視為一種民間英雄。總統不斷鼓勵公眾改變投資策略，轉向金融資產；最近他甚至將自己的公寓出租，以便用所得資金購買股票。
若外資持續流入，並配合穩定及其他措施，紀錄上最長的兩日賣壓——韓國股市Kospi在周二和周三下跌超過18%——或許不會被視為結構性退出，而是被視為政策支持的短期下跌，值得買入。
如果出售這些履歷，那將會挑戰最近的觀點，也就是所謂的“韓國折扣”現象——即韓國指數（Kospi）的估值遠低於其大多數同行——已經接近終結了。
South Korean stocks are advancing as foreign investors return and President Lee Jae Myung signals firmer support for markets. Those two dynamics need to remain in place for Thursday’s recovery to prove sustainable.

Overseas investors actually rushed in during Wednesday’s record slump for Korean equities. They snapped up ~$957m of the shares amidst a brutal rout that sent the benchmark Kospi down 12%, the most ever for a gauge that dates back to 1980. Overseas investors snapped up shares on the Kosdaq market for smaller companies at an unprecedented pace.

Wednesday’s capitulation may have reset valuations enough to attract bargain hunters from abroad. Thursday’s 12% surge in the Kospi at the open signals domestic investors may be joining that move too.

Many Korean retail investors regard Lee as something of a folk hero. The president, who has repeatedly urged the public to shift their investing strategy into financial assets, recently put his own put his own apartment up for sale to buy stocks.

If foreign inflows persist and Seoul follows through with stabilization and other measures, the biggest 2-day selloff on record -- the Kospi lost more than 18% over Tuesday and Wednesday -- may be viewed not as a structural exit, but as a policy-supported dip worth buying. If selling resumes then that would challenge the recent narrative that the days of the so-called Korea discount -- the Kospi’s valuations sit way below most of its peers -- are numbered. @parisprogram

化石燃料的風險與收益比例嚴重失衡，價格呈上升趨勢——儘管美國承諾恢復霍爾木兹水道的通航，原油價格仍在周四大幅上漲。目前尚無明確的時間表來結束這場衝突，而且即便爭議得到解決，伊朗的局勢可能依然會持續混亂，這將導致該國石油供應及通過這條關鍵水道的交通狀況持續處於不確定狀態。

Investors look to be treating the US-Israeli war on Iran as a risk blip similar to those that occurred over the past year under President Trump. That level of complacency means global equities -- and US stocks in particular -- are in danger of suffering steep declines in the event that oil prices remain elevated or surge higher again as the conflict drags on.

The risk-reward for fossil fuels is skewed to higher prices -- crude is climbing substantially Thursday despite US promises to get the Strait of Hormuz working again. There’s no clear timeline for an end to hostilities, with the potential that even if a resolution is found the situation in Iran will remain chaotic enough to sustain uncertainty around the country’s oil supplies and traffic through the critical waterway. Each day of stasis increases the risk of oilfields to be shut in, and switching them back on at a later date would take time.

There are clear parallels to 2022, and so far one of those is the path for crude, though Russia’s substantially larger role in the global oil market back then compared with Iran’s now means oil’s gains this time around have been somewhat less rapid. Brent rose 25% from Dec. 31, 2021, to the eve of the Ukraine invasion on Feb. 23, whereas it gained 19% this year before the past weekend’s strikes. While the jump in 2026 lags 2022’s, the gap is far from wide. With Brent almost 40% up YTD we are already in the ballpark for the sustained elevation reached in 2022.
Investors may want to be wary of the lessons from the Ukraine war, and from the US’s move into Iraq in 2003. Conflicts can last much longer than they expect -- and that oil costs will linger at levels much higher than anticipated by businesses when we entered 2026 amid talk of a sustained crude glut.

The rush to buy dips in equities rests on the experience of the gains reaped by those who piled in after previous sudden declines in stocks. But the long-term dynamics for oil and the Middle East along with recent history offers some caution. @parisprogram

Traders in Australia’s bonds seem to be singing from a different hymn sheet to their peers in cash-rate futures heading into this month’s policy review from the central bank.

The yield on three-year bonds has continued to climb relentlessly since October, and picked up pace this week after Governor Michele Bullock sounded particularly hawkish by warning that the March 17 meeting was live -- debunking the notion that the Reserve Bank of Australia moves only at a quarterly pace.

Despite her warning, cash-rate futures are pricing in just a tail risk for an increase this month. While it is possible that the RBA will hold rates, it strikes me that the chance of a move is lot more than the 24% the futures markets assigns.

The 3y/10y segment of the bond curve has collapsed to just 43bps from more than 100bps seen just about a year ago, in line with the argument for global bonds. Bonds in Europe still face downside risk today despite the thaw in market sentiment overnight.

Yields on front-end securities will likely push higher again as there is no material incentive for traders to bid bonds where little else — apart from the market mood — has changed. Brent crude prices are up again and a lot higher than we have known in recent times, and should the conflict continue, central bank forecasts on inflation for the year that were predicated on much lower oil and natural-gas prices will soon be outdated.

While overnight indexed swaps in the UK have moved away from the idea of an interest-rate cut this month, they are still pricing about 35 basis points of loosening from the Bank of England in the remainder of the year. Even if the conflict in the Middle East were to continue for the just a few more weeks, the disruption to energy supplies may last sufficiently long as to keep inflation elevated through the remainder of the year. The BOE won’t find that backdrop optimal to cut rates, so the optimism about the path of easing appears misplaced.

Pricing on the European Central Bank is far more realistic. Although the markets aren’t assigning any meaningful chance of a rate hike this year, they aren’t pricing a reduction either. Given that inflation in the euro zone is well anchored, that pricing is appropriate.

But we are still in the first phase of bond-market adjustment. There is a unifying flattening theme beyond the day as well, which I explored here. @parisprogram

我們已經看到歐洲央行（ECB）對未來利率走勢的預測發生快速變化，市場從年初認為年底有約 40% 的降息可能性，轉而認為升息的可能性高達 34%。這是因為交易員們對能源成本上升可能帶來的通膨影響感到擔憂。自然而然地，這一情況會讓人聯想到 2022 年俄烏戰爭期間的經歷——當時歐洲央行為應對能源成本激增，實施了 450 基點（bps）的緊縮政策。
然而，當時歐洲央行（ECB）的存款利率設定在-0.5%，這表明貨幣政策已經相當寬鬆，無法有效應對如此巨大的價格衝擊，因此需要進行一定的調整以追上通貨膨脹的發展。四年後，存款利率上升至2%。在此之前，這一利率在某些方面被認為過於嚴格，歐洲央行存在未能實現其通貨膨脹目標的風險。
即使本周五年的通膨掉期利率有所回升，市場對歐洲央行（ECB）的通脹管理目標已經持接近的態度，並未明顯超出其目標範圍。此外，歐洲央行理事會在考慮是否要進一步加強對經濟增長衝擊的應對措施時也持謹慎態度；而歐洲央行主席Villeroy最近的言論也表明他們並不急於採取行動。
因此，雖然市場預期歐洲央行（ECB）今年不會再進行降息是合理的，但對於明確升息的押注可能會對目前的資訊發展產生過度反應。
Oil’s move lower is fading fast, and European natural gas futures have opened higher by about 9%. Both are pushing Eurozone bond yields higher in early trading, while investors are continuing to add to bets on policy tightening this year from the ECB.

They should be wary about overdoing the latter though.

We’ve already seen a rapid repricing of the ECB’s outlook, with markets having flipped from assigning a ~40% chance of an rate cut by year-end to as much as a ~34% chance of a hike, as traders fret over the inflation implications from a surge in energy costs.

Naturally, parallels will be drawn with the 2022 Russia-Ukraine conflict which saw the ECB deliver a 450bps tightening cycle in response to surging energy costs. However, back then, the starting point for the ECB deposit rate at -0.5% meant that policy was already far too loose to deal with such a price shock and an element of catch up was required.

Fast forward four years and the deposit rate is 2%. Prior to this week, that had been deemed too restrictive in some quarters, with the ECB at risk of undershooting its inflation mandate. Even the pick-up in five year-inflation swaps this week has brought expectations in proximity to the ECB’s mandate, not materially above it.

There will also be reticence on the Governing Council to add further weight to a potential economic growth shock, and recent remarks from ECB’s Villeroy suggest a lack of urgency.

So while it makes sense for markets to price out any more easing from the ECB this year, betting on outright hikes risks being an overreaction to the current newsflow. @parisprogram

這種背景如今已處於風險之中。伊朗的衝突使得歐洲央行（ECB）可能採取更加激進的貨幣政策。同時，更高的能源成本將對經濟增長造成壓力，進一步惡化法國的財政狀況。這種貿易失衡與通貨緊縮並存的局面對高度負債的國家來說並不有利。當市場開始關注財政問題時，往往會首先對那些有過財政失誤紀錄的國家造成影響。
法國在財政紀律方面的表現，包括在俄烏能源衝突期間對家庭提供大量直接支持，進一步強化了其脆弱性。若能源價格持續高漲，政治壓力重複類似措施的可能性將增加，這將進一步加壓法國的財政狀況。
進入這場危機時，市場預測2026年的財政赤字將佔GDP的約5.3%，遠超歐洲委員會設定的3%的目標。貨幣市場的借貸活動需要穩定的風險情緒和低波動性作爲基礎。雖然目前市場波動仍相對較小，但法國債券收益率的風險卻明顯上升。
French bonds look particularly vulnerable to selloff if the Iran conflict continues.

French spreads had tightened in recent months largely on the absence of negative headlines rather than on any meaningful improvement in fundamentals. Low volatility and an ECB that appeared anchored at 2% for the foreseeable future encouraged investors to pick up carry in France, and spreads compressed back to levels seen before the 2024 snap election.

That backdrop is now at risk. The conflict in Iran raises the possibility of a more hawkish ECB. At the same time, higher energy costs would weigh on growth, worsening France’s debt sustainability dynamics. A stagflationary mix is not friendly to highly indebted sovereigns.

When markets pivot toward fiscal concerns, they tend to punish those with a history of slippage first. France’s track record on fiscal discipline, including substantial direct support to households during the Russia-Ukraine energy shock, reinforces that vulnerability. If energy prices remain elevated, political pressure to repeat such measures would likely increase.

That would heap more pressure on France’s finances. Going into this crisis, consensus forecasts put the 2026 budget deficit at around 5.3% of GDP, well above the European Commission’s 3% benchmark.

Carry trades depend on stable risk sentiment and low volatility. While market moves remain relatively contained for now, the risks for French yields appear skewed higher. @parisprogram

Japanese government bonds also look among those at risk of an outsized selloff on the conflict in the Middle East.

Japan relies on the Middle East for more than 90% of its crude imports, much of it coming through the Strait of Hormuz, and the surge in oil will compound the problem of accelerating wage growth. Rengo, the nation’s biggest trade union, is demanding wage growth of almost 6%, way above levels that would be consistent with a stable Bank of Japan price target. Unions representing smaller firms are seeking even more.

The BOJ is already well behind the curve in taming inflation, and at a mere 0.75%, its benchmark rate trails estimates of the neutral rate by about 75 basis points. But given the government’s extra spending, likely pay hikes for this year and the prospect of spiraling crude prices, the central bank will have to take its rates much farther beyond neutral. And the longer the BOJ drags its feet, the worse the prognosis for JGBs. Policymakers meet later this month, but our colleague report that there is little chance of an immediate move.

The yield on two-year JGBs has climbed today, but at 1.26%, the markets are remarkably too sanguine about the pace of rate hikes needed. By the end of the year, there is a good chance that yields will be much higher.

天然氣價格再次飆升，如果政府不能迅速加以控制，這在政治上將是極其危險的。這對美國來說並非好事；大宗商品價格的上升總會引發通貨膨脹的擔憂。這次的地緣政治風險使得下任聯邦儲備系統（Federal Reserve）主席更難按計劃降息了。
市場基準的聯邦儲備系統利率預估在年底將大幅上揚，目前已達到九個月來的高點。美國債券收益率曾大幅下跌，但這種趨勢已經逆轉。對美國來說，這場危機在經濟條件並不特別緊縮的情況下就已經化解了。對亞洲及其他發展中國家來說，任何美元走強的跡象都可能成為財政困難的挑戰。
自 EM 以來，表現優異的股市在美国市場中保持了穩定的走勢。然而，隨著伊朗首份相關報告的公佈，這種優勢迅速消退，其消退的速度和程度令人印象深刻。
In markets, America has been the winner of the Middle East conflict so far, John Authers writes.

That’s in part because of positioning, with the rest of the world enjoying its strongest streak of outperformance versus the US in years. The war created the grounds for a reversal. Also, the dollar still functions as a safe haven.

By contrast, Europe has barely had time to recover from the energy crisis after the Ukraine invasion of 2022. Natural gas prices have spiked again, and that’s hugely dangerous politically for governments if not reversed quickly.

This isn’t all good for the US. Rising commodity prices will always arouse fears of inflation. This latest dose of geopolitical risk makes it far harder for the next chairman of the Federal Reserve to cut rates as advertised. Market-based estimates of the fed funds rate at year-end have jumped and are now as high as they’ve been in nine months. Treasury yields had been falling dramatically, and that has also reversed.
Thankfully for the US, this crisis has broken when conditions were not particularly tight. There might be more of an issue for emerging markets. Any strength for the greenback is taken as a problem for developing countries trying to finance their debt. EM had enjoyed an impressive run of late, with stock markets comfortably outperforming the US. The speed and ferocity with which that flipped after the first reports from Iran has been something to behold. @parisprogram
